name: Old South Meeting House
description: The great meeting hall blazes with tallow candles and the heat of five thousand bodies packed into its wooden pews — the air smells of wool, tallow smoke, and barely contained fury. Samuel Adams stands at the front, his voice cutting through the din like a bell, while men press close to hear every word.
atmosphericDetails: Candle wax has dripped in long yellow rivers down the gallery rails, hardening into formations that look like frozen waterfalls.
Two men near the back door are speaking in urgent whispers, their heads so close together their hat brims touch.
A broadsheet nailed to the pillar reads 'NO TAXATION WITHOUT REPRESENTATION' in letters large enough to read across the room.
